results.rendered_html:
jeh (cf. √jabh, jṛmbh) cl. 1. Ā. (p. jehamāna) to open the mouth, breathe heavily, be excessively thirsty,  RV. i, 163, 6 ; x ;
to gape, i, 110, 5 ;
to move (‘to strive after’,  Vop.  &  Sāy. ),  Naigh. ii.

results.rendered_html:
√jeh , jehate  NAIGH. 2,14  (gatikarman).  DHĀTUP. 16,43  (prayatne streben, sich bemühen).
1〉 den Mund aufsperren, schnauben; lechzen: asya śuṣmāso dadṛśānapaverjehamānasya svanayanniyudbhiḥ  ṚV. 10,3,6 . śiro apaśyaṃ pathibhiḥ sugobhirareṇubhirjehamānaṃ patatri 1,163,6 . (pitaraḥ) ye tātṛṣurdevatrā jehamānā hotrāvida stomataṣṭāsa arkaiḥ 10,15,9 .
— 2〉 gähnen, klaffen: kṣetramiva vi mamustejanena~ ekaṃ pātramṛbhavo jehamānam vas hians  ṚV. 1,110,5 . — Verwandt mit jambh, jrambh; vgl. edh und ardh, geha und gṛha, geṇhai und gṛhṇāti.
— vi den Rachen aufsperren: vijehamānaḥ paraśurna jiḥvāṃ dravirna drāvayati dāru dhakṣat  ṚV. 6,3,4 .

jeh 1 A. (jehate) Ved.
1 To reach, go towards.
2 To strive after, exert.
3 To open the mouth, pant, gasp.
